name: Html.Parser

unions.name: Node
unions.comment:
An html node is tree of text, comments, and element nodes. An element (e.g. `
hello
`) can have attributes and child nodes.

aliases.name: Document
aliases.comment: An html document has a `<!doctype>` and then a root html node.

values.name: documentToPrettyString
values.comment: Convert a document into a pretty, indented string.

values.name: documentToString
values.comment: Convert a document into a string starting with `<!doctype html>` followed by the root html node.

values.name: nodeToHtml
values.comment: Turn a single node into an Elm html node that Elm can render.

values.name: nodeToPrettyString
values.comment: Generate a pretty string for a single html node.

values.name: nodeToString
values.comment: Convert an html node into a non-pretty string. nodeToString (Element "a" [] [ Text "hi" ]) == "hi"

values.name: nodesToHtml
values.comment:
Turn a multiple html nodes into Elm html that Elm can render. view : Html Msg view = Html.div [] ("

hello world

" |> Html.Parser.run |> Result.map Html.Parser.nodesToHtml |> Result.withDefault [ Html.text "parse error" ] )

values.name: nodesToPrettyString
values.comment: Turn a node tree into a pretty-printed, indented html string. ("hello" |> Html.Parser.run |> Result.map nodesToPrettyString ) == Ok """ hello """

values.name: nodesToString
values.comment:
Convert multiple html nodes into a non-pretty string. nodesToString [ Element "a" [] [ Text "hi" ] , Element "div" [] [ Element "span" [] [] ] ] == "hi
"

values.name: run
values.comment:
Parse an html fragment into a list of html nodes. The html fragment can have multiple top-level nodes. run "
hi
bye
" == Ok [ Element "div" [] [ Text "hi" ] , Element "div" [] [ Text "bye" ] ]

values.name: runDocument
values.comment:
Parses `<!doctype html>` and any html nodes after.

Always returns a single root node. Wraps nodes in a root `<html>` node if one is not present.

**Caveat**: If there are multiple top-level nodes and one of them is `<html>`, then this
function will wrap them all in another `<html>` node.

values.name: runElement
values.comment:
Like `run` except it only succeeds when the html input is a
single top-level element, and it always returns a single node.
